title: Apicra atrovirens
taxonomy: Apicra atrovirens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apicraceae. It is native to the Americas, specifically the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Apicra atrovirens plant belongs to the order Zingiberales and is a member of the family Apicraceae, which is a small and rare family of flowering plants. intro: Apicra atrovirens, also known as Night-Blooming Cereus, is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its nocturnal flowering habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Apicra atrovirens include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Apicra atrovirens is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory problems.
